title: Graphs, Representations, and Traversal · Spiral Review
summary: Model service dependencies, social links, and routes without losing direction or weight. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a graph library with BFS and DFS traces after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define vertices, edges, direction, weights, self-loops, and parallel-edge policy from the domain.
Choose adjacency lists, matrices, edge lists, or implicit neighbors from density and operations.
Maintain BFS frontier and discovery invariants for unweighted shortest paths and layers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Graph Model correctness-and-cost proof for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Graph Model assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Analyze directed edges A→B, A→C, B→D, C→D, and D→B. Report traversal order separately from reachability, return a cycle witness, and reject invalid topological claims.
